summary: Matched a large batch of CPU/common fighter logic in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c, with supporting type/header/symbol fixes. The decomp.dev report credited the PR with +10340 matched bytes, 22 new full matches, and 4 improved unmatched functions, all in ftCo_0A01. The work replaced several placeholder asm markers with C, improved struct field knowledge for Fighter_x1A88_t and collision color types, and corrected prototypes that were blocking matches.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; gained most of the matched C implementations and cleanup.
Refined Fighter and Fighter_x1A88_t fields used by the new matches.
Updated local static prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete types.
Retyped color data and pointer use to GXColor to support matching collision-related calls.
Updated lbColl_800096B4 and lbColl_80009DD4 prototypes to typed GXColor/Vec3 arguments.
Changed ft_80087A80 return type from s8 to int.
Kept the ft_80087A80 declaration consistent with the C implementation.
Added a TODO noting possible Fighter_GObj* typing for gm_8016C6C0, as seen from ftCo_800A53DC.
Renamed several data symbols at 0x803C5BE8/0x803C5C10/0x803C5C98/0x803C5CE8/0x803C5D60 to jtbl_*.

what_changed: Implemented previously placeholder-marked functions including ftCo_800A0148, ftCo_800A08F0, ftCo_800A53DC, ftCo_800A5ACC, ftCo_800A5CE0, ftCo_800A5F4C, ftCo_800A8EB0, ftCo_800A96B8, and ftCo_800B395C.

Improved or completed smaller functions including ftCo_800A0CB0, ftCo_800A0DA4, ftCo_800A0F00, ftCo_800A17E4, ftCo_800A1AB4, ftCo_800A3554, ftCo_800A3710, ftCo_800A49B4, ftCo_800A4A40, ftCo_800A50D4, ftCo_800A80E4, ftCo_800AD42C, ftCo_800AFC40, ftCo_800B0760, ftCo_800B0CA8, and ftCo_800B0E98 according to the bot report/diff.
Added comments identifying ftCo_803C5A68 as an ItemKind priority table for CPU item targeting, with higher numbers preferred.
Changed ftCo_803C6594 declaration from StageBlastZone** to StageBlastZone* array syntax.
Replaced M2C_FIELD accesses for Fighter_x1A88_t offsets 0x55C, 0x560, 0x564, and 0x568 with named float fields x55C, x560, x564, and x568.
Split unknown Fighter_x1A88_t padding into discovered fields x2C, x30, x6C Vec2, x74 Vec2, and a two-bit field xFA_b34.
Changed Fighter smash_attrs.x2134_vibrateFrame from s8 to u8 and changed ft_80087A80 to return int, matching a note in the diff that ftCo_800A0F00 matched if ft_80087A80 returned int.
Retyped collision color globals and parameters from raw u8 arrays/pointers to GXColor, using .a alpha-field access instead of byte index [3].
Updated symbols.txt so several table-like data labels use jtbl_* instead of ftCo_*.

Added helper/inlining variants such as ftCo_800A2170_it, ftCo_IsAlly_dontinline, inlineD0_it, itemDist, inlineI1, inlineI1_alt, and inlineI2 to express repeated logic while preserving matching behavior.
smart_moves: Used concrete struct fields to remove M2C_FIELD hacks in ftCo_800A0DA4, making the hurt-capsule extent computation readable while also matching.
Factored fighter-to-fighter distance into ftCo_800A1AB4 and reused it where doing so matched, while in another spot kept an explicit sqrtf(dx * dx + dy * dy) form where needed.
Introduced item-specific analogues of fighter helper logic, such as ftCo_800A2170_it and inlineD0_it, rather than forcing item pointers through fighter-shaped code.
Preserved known matching quirks with explicit comments, for example the repeated GET_ITEM calls in ftCo_800A5F4C marked as a stack padding hack.
Used #pragma dont_inline around ftCo_800A96B8, evidence that inlining control was necessary for codegen.
Converted collision color code to GXColor pointers and .a member access, which clarified intent and avoided raw byte indexing while supporting matches.
Renamed jump-table data symbols to jtbl_* in config, improving future symbol searchability and reducing misleading ftCo_* object names.
Documented item targeting priority semantics at the table definition, giving future AI/item decompilation work a useful anchor.
decomp_lessons: If a near-match depends on a callee return type, try widening/narrowing the prototype to the ABI-observed use site; here ft_80087A80 changed from s8 to int after a TODO noted that ftCo_800A0F00 matched with an int return.
Do not leave raw padding when a function reveals field semantics: discovered Fighter_x1A88_t offsets were promoted to int, float, Vec2, bitfield, and extent floats.
For color-like 4-byte globals, GXColor can be the correct type even when earlier code used u8[4]; member access to alpha can produce better C and matching.
Small control-flow rewrites from early returns to else-if/else blocks can matter for matching without changing behavior.
Artificial helper wrappers with names like *_dontinline or item-specific inline variants can be a valid matching tactic when the compiler's inlining decisions are sensitive.
When a data object is a jump table, use jtbl_* naming in symbols.txt instead of leaving it under a function-like/data prefix.
Document stack padding or repeated-read hacks directly at the site so future cleanups know the odd shape is intentional.
Use typed entity pointers for item-vs-fighter target logic; this PR corrected x4C use in ftCo_800A3710 as an Item* and used ip->entity for item gobj calls.
naming_conventions: Jump-table data symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt should use jtbl_803C....
Static ftCo functions remain address-based ftCo_800A.... names until semantic names are known.
Temporary inline helpers may use descriptive suffixes indicating their matching role or argument domain, e.g. ftCo_IsAlly_dontinline, ftCo_800A2170_it, inlineD0_it.
Discovered but still semantically unknown Fighter_x1A88_t fields keep offset-style names such as x2C, x30, x55C, x560, x564, and x568.
ItemKind-based code uses It_Kind_* enum names, with a comment that It_Kind_L_Gun_Ray was used as a sentinel for any common item in ftCo_800A5F4C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed placeholder markers like /// #ftCo_800A0148, /// #ftCo_800A08F0, /// #ftCo_800A53DC, /// #ftCo_800A5ACC, /// #ftCo_800A5CE0, /// #ftCo_800A5F4C, /// #ftCo_800A8EB0, /// #ftCo_800A96B8, and /// #ftCo_800B395C by replacing them with C.
Changed ft_80087A80's return type to int to satisfy matching in ftCo_800A0F00.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop for ftCo_800A96B8.
Rewrote cubic random expression inline in ftCo_800A0CB0 instead of using inlineA0: x = x * x * x; rand = 1.0F - x.
Used explicit float constants and divisions by 127.0F or 128.0F in analog-stick conversion helpers to get correct codegen.
Retained a deliberate repeated GET_ITEM sequence in ftCo_800A5F4C with a TODO calling it a stack padding hack.
Replaced byte-index alpha checks like temp_r3[3] == 0xFF with GXColor alpha checks like temp_r3->a == 0xFF.
Used small inline wrappers and alternate helper variants to control inlining and expression shape.

review_feedback.summary: Automated report showed matched code increased to 39.51%, +0.27%, +10340 bytes.
Reported 22 new full matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01 and 4 additional improvements in unmatched functions.
No human review comments or PR body were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_800A5F4C or document CPU item targeting behavior.
Check whether gm_8016C6C0's parameter was later corrected to Fighter_GObj* or remains Item_GObj*/HSD_GObj-shaped.
Search for follow-up cleanups to the repeated GET_ITEM stack padding hack in ftCo_800A5F4C.
Search later Fighter_x1A88_t field naming work for semantic names for x2C, x30, x55C, x560, x564, and x568.
Find other PRs that convert collision color u8 arrays to GXColor for matching.
